name: Cardeña y El Cerezo – circular through the Cardeña y Montoro Sierra Natural Park
name_original: Cardeña y El Cerezo – circular por el Parque Natural de la Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro
region: National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro
region_intro: Jogging around National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro offers a diverse environment for runners. The region is characterized by rolling hills, granite boulder outcrops, and a mix of oak woodlands and Mediterranean scrubland. River valleys, including the Yeguas and Arenoso, carve through the landscape, providing varied terrain. This topography ensures a range of inclines and declines suitable for different running intensities. Best jogging routes around National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro The most popular jogging route is Yeguas River Meander - Obejo Bridge Loop, a 11.9 miles (19.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the scenic meanders of the Yeguas River. Another top favourite among local runners is Cardeña and Tejoneras Reservoir Loop, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.5 km) path. This route offers views of the Tejoneras Reservoir and surrounding woodlands. Local runners also love the Cardeña y El Cerezo – circular through the Cardeña y Montoro Sierra Natural Park, a 13.9 miles (22.3 km) trail leading through dehesa woodlands and past granite formations, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Jogging around National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro is defined by rolling hills, dehesa pastureland, and oak woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 1.7 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro?
A: There are over 20 dedicated running routes in National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro. These routes offer a variety of distances and difficulty levels, catering to different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro?
A: The park features a diverse landscape ideal for running, characterized by gentle, undulating hills, granite boulder outcrops, and a mix of dehesa pastureland and extensive oak woodlands. You'll encounter varied underfoot conditions, from open grasslands to shaded forest paths and river valleys, providing both challenging inclines and more relaxed sections.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in the park?
A: Yes, while many routes offer moderate to difficult challenges, there are options suitable for beginners. The park's 'undulating hills' mean that even easier paths will have some gentle inclines. Look for routes described as 'moderate' on komoot, as these often provide a good balance for those new to trail running or seeking a less strenuous experience. Currently, there is 1 easy route and 11 moderate routes listed.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while running?
A: Runners can enjoy unique geological formations like rounded granite blocks known as 'bolos', especially along trails such as the scenic Cardeña–El Cerezo trail. The park also boasts significant dehesa pastureland, extensive oak woodlands (including Pyrenean oak near Aldea del Cerezo), and picturesque river valleys like those carved by the Yeguas and Arenoso rivers. You might also spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5341650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obejo Bridge over the Yeguas River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly scenic or offer great views?
A: Many routes offer scenic beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1303820764/cardena-and-tejoneras-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardeña and Tejoneras Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides lovely views of the reservoir and surrounding woodlands. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001078236/cardena-y-el-cerezo-circular-through-the-cardena-y-montoro-sierra-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardeña y El Cerezo – circular through the Cardeña y Montoro Sierra Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; takes you through dehesa woodlands and past striking granite formations. For panoramic vistas, consider routes near viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4363470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polar Viewpoint – Aldea del Cerezo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5354373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Astronomical Observation Point&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in Spanish national parks, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife, especially given the presence of species like the Iberian lynx and wolf. Always check specific park regulations or trail signage before heading out, as some areas might have restrictions during certain seasons or for conservation purposes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304473999/yeguas-river-meander-obejo-bridge-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yeguas River Meander - Obejo Bridge Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a full circuit of the scenic river meanders.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro?
A: The best times for running are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in Andalusia, making early mornings or late evenings preferable if running during this season. Winters are generally mild, but some higher elevation paths might be cooler or occasionally wet.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in National Park Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 1.7 stars from over 3 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse environment, from rolling hills and granite formations to the peaceful oak woodlands and Mediterranean scrubland. The varied topography ensures a good mix of inclines and declines, making it suitable for different running intensities.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or water features?
A: Yes, the park is divided by several rivers like the Yeguas and Arenoso, and features reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1303820764/cardena-and-tejoneras-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardeña and Tejoneras Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically offers views of the Tejoneras Reservoir. The Yeguas River also provides lush, scenic routes along its banks.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails?
A: Specific parking information varies by trailhead. However, the Visitor Centre Venta Nueva, located just outside Cardeña, serves as a good starting point for exploring the park's trails and likely offers parking facilities. For individual routes, check the route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking options.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. The park offers several difficult routes for experienced runners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304473999/yeguas-river-meander-obejo-bridge-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yeguas River Meander - Obejo Bridge Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 11.9-mile (19.1 km) trail with significant elevation changes, exploring the scenic meanders of the Yeguas River. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmolejo Spa – Pilgrim's Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 22.6 km route with over 590 meters of elevation gain.
tips: The low light pollution in this place has made it a regular meeting point for professionals and amateur astronomy.
Quiet and aesthetic road along which you can easily walk, although there are parallel paths for the most part.

Cardeña is a charming municipality located in the province of Córdoba, in the heart of the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park. 
In addition, Cardeña is known for its rich gastronomy and natural surroundings, making it a perfect destination for rural tourism.
